event: Allegory of March - Detail from Palazzo Schifanoia Frescoes
symbols: scepter or wand — authority or the directing force of the season
ring held in hand — a token of allegiance or cyclical time
red stockings and courtly dress — aristocratic association with seasonal festivities
description: A fresco detail showing a single standing figure of a young person with curly golden hair, wearing a pink-red tunic over white sleeves and bright red stockings or hose. The figure holds a long staff or scepter in the right hand and a ring or circular object in the left. The background is a faded greenish-blue. The style is flat and decorative, typical of Italian Renaissance fresco cycles. The figure stands frontally on a simple ground.

_meta.title: Allegory of March – Triumph of Minerva and Sign of Aries. Frescos in Palazzo Schifanoia (detail)
